rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong).
Source-verification maintainer block on any student-facing file with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:29: '**The paraphrase I shipped as a quote.** ... Source verification against our research observations found Charles's actual line: *"The models were good enough. The harness wasn't."*' — Geoff Charles named with specific quote in student-facing body; no source-verification section in maintainer block (no URL list, no freshness re-check, no fallback framing).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a `**Source verification — MUST DO before first cohort:**` section in the maintainer block listing the Geoff Charles URL (x.com/geoffintech/status/2042002590758572377) and the verbatim quote to confirm at delivery time. Prior audit (ae101-audit-lectures.md) accepted the meta-narrative framing as a 'strong move'; source-verify section is still required per §11 when a named practitioner + specific quote appear in student-facing body.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed Claude Code Skills invoke by name, never by path.

platform_claims_checked.claim: A capability check read the current Claude Code documentation: `/schedule` is Routines, remote, cloud-based. Desktop local tasks are a separate primitive.

citations_checked.claim: Source verification against our research observations found Charles's actual line: "The models were good enough. The harness wasn't."
